section_title: Required setback distance - flat-roof building envelope (R1 through R5)

source.document_currency_banner_note: City of Yes for Housing Opportunity moved the R1-R5 residential bulk (height and setback) regulations into the ZR 23-42 series with a Last Amended date of 12/5/2024. The site-wide currency banner was not captured for this retrieval and is a needs_review item.
source.verification_required: Draft capture of ZR 23-423 supplied by official-source-researcher (access 2026-07-22, zr.planning.nyc.gov). It has NOT been re-verified byte-for-byte against raw HTML by the rules-engineer, so raw_html_verified is false and extraction_status is extracted_draft. The governing section, the exact heights/depths, the district-to-statement association, and every applicability condition (building type, street width, qualifying-residential-site geography, special-district/overlay/historic-district context) MUST be confirmed by a qualified zoning professional (G6) before any rule citing this snapshot is published or Verified.

verbatim_excerpt: A setback shall be provided in accordance with this Section. The depth of the setback shall be at least 10 feet from a street wall fronting on a wide street, and at least 15 feet from a street wall fronting on a narrow street.
notes: Supplies the setback DEPTH above the base height for the flat-roof envelope: at least 10 ft on a wide street, at least 15 ft on a narrow street (wide/narrow per §12-10). The depth is CONDITIONAL on street width; the canonical property_profile exposes NO street-width field, so the setback-depth constraint fails closed to professional_review_required.
This excerpt is the official-source-researcher reading of §23-423 pending byte-level raw-HTML verbatim confirmation (a needs_review item).
Captured for M4-T006 as a governing-source candidate for the R5-series height & setback DRAFT rule family (needs_review).
Nothing in this record is a Verified zoning determination. Verified requires an M4 published rule plus G6 qualified-human approval (owner directive item 5).
